2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"sync"
6		"time"
7	)
8	
9	type heartbeatStore interface {
10		TouchClaimHeartbeat(string) error
11	}
12	
13	// WithHeartbeat runs fn while periodically calling store.TouchClaimHeartbeat
14	// on a background goroutine at the given interval. When reporter is non-nil,
15	// reporter.OnTick is invoked after each TouchClaimHeartbeat so callers can
16	// mirror the same liveness signal to a worker-status sidecar and the
17	// optional server probe without rewriting the bead tracker. The goroutine
18	// is stopped and waited for before WithHeartbeat returns.
19	func WithHeartbeat[T any](ctx context.Context, beadID string, interval time.Duration, store heartbeatStore, reporter LivenessReporter, fn func() (T, error)) (T, error) {
20		ticker := time.NewTicker(interval)
21		defer ticker.Stop()
22		return withHeartbeatCh(ctx, beadID, ticker.C, store, reporter, fn)
23	}
24	
25	// withHeartbeatCh is the injectable variant used by tests to supply a stub tick channel.
26	func withHeartbeatCh[T any](ctx context.Context, beadID string, tickCh <-chan time.Time, store heartbeatStore, reporter LivenessReporter, fn func() (T, error)) (T, error) {
27		hbCtx, hbCancel := context.WithCancel(ctx)
28		var wg sync.WaitGroup
29		wg.Add(1)
30		go func() {
31			defer wg.Done()
32			for {
33				select {
34				case <-hbCtx.Done():
35					return
36				case tick := <-tickCh:
37					_ = store.TouchClaimHeartbeat(beadID)
38					if reporter != nil {
39						reporter.OnTick(tick)
40					}
41				}
42			}
43		}()
44		result, err := fn()
45		hbCancel()
46		wg.Wait()
47		return result, err
48	}

1	package work
2	
3	import (
4		"encoding/json"
5		"io"

8		"time"
9	
10		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/workerstatus"
11	)
12	
13	// LivenessReporter observes worker liveness ticks. WithHeartbeat invokes
14	// OnTick after each TouchClaimHeartbeat so the caller can mirror the same
15	// signal to a worker-status sidecar and to the optional server probe
16	// without rewriting the bead tracker.
17	type LivenessReporter interface {
18		OnTick(now time.Time)
19	}
20	
21	// SidecarLivenessReporter is the production LivenessReporter. Each tick it
22	// (a) writes a status.json sidecar under .ddx/workers/<worker-id>/ via the
23	// workerstatus package, and (b) emits a worker.heartbeat envelope to sink
24	// (typically the loop EventSink, which is teed to the workerprobe). The
25	// sidecar write is best-effort and never fails the loop; the sink emit is
26	// best-effort and never blocks the heartbeat goroutine.
27	type SidecarLivenessReporter struct {
28		mu          sync.Mutex
29		projectRoot string
30		rec         workerstatus.LivenessRecord
31		sink        io.Writer
32		sessionID   string
33	}
34	
35	// NewSidecarLivenessReporter constructs a SidecarLivenessReporter for the
36	// given worker. projectRoot must be the project's git root (where .ddx/
37	// lives). sink may be nil for callers that don't want event mirroring.
38	func NewSidecarLivenessReporter(projectRoot, workerID, sessionID string, sink io.Writer) *SidecarLivenessReporter {
39		return &SidecarLivenessReporter{
40			projectRoot: projectRoot,
41			sink:        sink,
42			sessionID:   sessionID,
43			rec: workerstatus.LivenessRecord{
44				WorkerID:    workerID,
45				ProjectRoot: projectRoot,
46				PID:         os.Getpid(),
47				StartedAt:   time.Now().UTC(),
48			},
49		}
50	}
51	
52	// SetAttempt records the in-flight attempt identity. Called once per claim
53	// before WithHeartbeat starts so the first tick records the right bead.
54	func (r *SidecarLivenessReporter) SetAttempt(beadID, attemptID, phase, route, harness, model, profile string, childPID int) {
55		if r == nil {
56			return
57		}
58		r.mu.Lock()
59		r.rec.CurrentBead = beadID
60		r.rec.AttemptID = attemptID
61		r.rec.Phase = phase
62		r.rec.Route = route
63		r.rec.Harness = harness
64		r.rec.Model = model
65		r.rec.Profile = profile
66		r.rec.ChildPID = childPID
67		r.mu.Unlock()
68	}
69	
70	// ClearAttempt drops the in-flight attempt identity once the attempt
71	// terminates. The worker_id and project_root remain so an idle drain loop
72	// still presents as the same worker between attempts.
73	func (r *SidecarLivenessReporter) ClearAttempt() {
74		if r == nil {
75			return
76		}
77		r.mu.Lock()
78		r.rec.CurrentBead = ""
79		r.rec.AttemptID = ""
80		r.rec.Phase = ""
81		r.rec.ChildPID = 0
82		r.mu.Unlock()
83	}
84	
85	// OnTick updates the sidecar with the new last_activity_at and mirrors a
86	// worker.heartbeat envelope to the configured sink (best-effort).
87	func (r *SidecarLivenessReporter) OnTick(now time.Time) {
88		if r == nil {
89			return
90		}
91		r.mu.Lock()
92		r.rec.LastActivityAt = now.UTC()
93		snapshot := r.rec
94		r.mu.Unlock()
95		_ = workerstatus.WriteLiveness(r.projectRoot, snapshot.WorkerID, snapshot)
96		r.emit(snapshot, now)
97	}
98	
99	// Snapshot returns a copy of the current sidecar record.
100	func (r *SidecarLivenessReporter) Snapshot() workerstatus.LivenessRecord {
101		r.mu.Lock()
102		defer r.mu.Unlock()
103		return r.rec
104	}
105	
106	func (r *SidecarLivenessReporter) emit(rec workerstatus.LivenessRecord, now time.Time) {
107		if r.sink == nil {
108			return
109		}
110		entry := map[string]any{
111			"session_id": r.sessionID,
112			"type":       "worker.heartbeat",
113			"ts":         now.UTC().Format(time.RFC3339Nano),
114			"data": map[string]any{
115				"worker_id":        rec.WorkerID,
116				"bead_id":          rec.CurrentBead,
117				"attempt_id":       rec.AttemptID,
118				"phase":            rec.Phase,
119				"route":            rec.Route,
120				"harness":          rec.Harness,
121				"model":            rec.Model,
122				"profile":          rec.Profile,
123				"last_activity_at": rec.LastActivityAt.Format(time.RFC3339Nano),
124			},
125		}
126		line, err := json.Marshal(entry)
127		if err != nil {
128			return
129		}
130		line = append(line, '\n')
131		_, _ = r.sink.Write(line)
132	}

75	// TestWorkerLiveness_ServerUnavailableDoesNotBlockWork verifies AC #3:
76	// with a nil sink (no server connection) the reporter still writes the
77	// sidecar, returns no error, and does not block the calling goroutine.
78	// The same must hold when sink.Write itself surfaces an error: liveness
79	// must remain best-effort and never propagate failure into the loop.
80	func TestWorkerLiveness_ServerUnavailableDoesNotBlockWork(t *testing.T) {
81		projectRoot := t.TempDir()
82	
83		// Case 1: nil sink (no server probe attached).
84		repNoSink := work.NewSidecarLivenessReporter(projectRoot, "wkr-no-sink", "sess", nil)
85		repNoSink.SetAttempt("ddx-1", "att-1", "running", "", "", "", "", 0)
86		repNoSink.OnTick(time.Now())
87		rec, err := workerstatus.ReadLiveness(projectRoot, "wkr-no-sink")
88		require.NoError(t, err, "sidecar must be written when no server sink is attached")
89		require.Equal(t, "ddx-1", rec.CurrentBead)
90	
91		// Case 2: failing sink (simulates a closed pipe / refused connection
92		// surfaced via TeeJSONL).
93		repFail := work.NewSidecarLivenessReporter(projectRoot, "wkr-fail-sink", "sess", failingWriter{})
94		repFail.SetAttempt("ddx-2", "att-2", "running", "", "", "", "", 0)
95		done := make(chan struct{})
96		go func() {
97			repFail.OnTick(time.Now())
98			close(done)
99		}()
100		select {
101		case <-done:
102		case <-time.After(2 * time.Second):
103			t.Fatal("OnTick blocked on a failing sink — liveness must be fail-open")
104		}
105		rec2, err := workerstatus.ReadLiveness(projectRoot, "wkr-fail-sink")
106		require.NoError(t, err)
107		require.Equal(t, "ddx-2", rec2.CurrentBead)
108	}

19	// carrying the Run() error.

21		t.Helper()

83				default:
84					close(started)
85				}
86				<-release

139	// TestWorkerHeartbeat_MirrorsPeriodicLivenessWithoutTrackerHeartbeatSpam
140	// verifies AC #1: a long-running ExecuteBeadWorker.Run with a short
141	// heartbeat interval refreshes worker-side liveness on every tick while
142	// the bead tracker bytes stay byte-identical between claim and terminal
143	// outcome. The sidecar's last_activity_at must advance across ticks so an
144	// operator-facing view can detect a live worker even when the tracker
145	// claim timestamp has not changed.
146	func TestWorkerHeartbeat_MirrorsPeriodicLivenessWithoutTrackerHeartbeatSpam(t *testing.T) {
147		store, first, _ := newExecuteLoopTestStore(t)
148		projectRoot := store.Dir
149		sessionID := "agent-loop-test-liveness"
150	
151		runtime := ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{
152			ProjectRoot: projectRoot,
153			SessionID:   sessionID,
154		}
155		release, done := runBlockedHeartbeatLoopWithRuntime(t, store, 5*time.Millisecond, runtime)
156	
157		trackerBefore, err := os.ReadFile(store.File)
158		require.NoError(t, err)
159	
160		// Wait through enough ticks to advance last_activity_at at least twice.
161		require.Eventually(t, func() bool {
162			rec, err := workerstatus.ReadLiveness(projectRoot, sessionID)
163			if err != nil {
164				return false
165			}
166			return rec.CurrentBead == first.ID && !rec.LastActivityAt.IsZero()
167		}, time.Second, 5*time.Millisecond, "sidecar must reflect the claimed bead")
168	
169		firstSnap, err := workerstatus.ReadLiveness(projectRoot, sessionID)
170		require.NoError(t, err)
171		require.Equal(t, first.ID, firstSnap.CurrentBead, "sidecar must record the claimed bead id")
172		require.NotEmpty(t, firstSnap.AttemptID, "sidecar must record a provisional attempt id")
173	
174		require.Eventually(t, func() bool {
175			rec, err := workerstatus.ReadLiveness(projectRoot, sessionID)
176			if err != nil {
177				return false
178			}
179			return rec.LastActivityAt.After(firstSnap.LastActivityAt)
180		}, time.Second, 5*time.Millisecond, "last_activity_at must advance on each heartbeat tick")
181	
182		trackerAfter, err := os.ReadFile(store.File)
183		require.NoError(t, err)
184		require.Equal(t, trackerBefore, trackerAfter,
185			"sidecar liveness updates must not rewrite beads.jsonl between claim and terminal outcome")
186	
187		release()
188		require.NoError(t, <-done)
189	}

+// TestWorkerStatusSidecar_RecordsCurrentBeadAttemptAndLastActivity
+// verifies AC #2: after several heartbeat ticks of a simulated attempt,
+// the .ddx/workers/<worker-id>/status.json sidecar JSON contains the
+// worker_id, current_bead, attempt_id (provisional or final), phase,
+// last_activity_at, and pid (when available). AC #3 is satisfied
+// implicitly by workerstatus.WriteLiveness's tmp-then-rename strategy
+// (concurrent readers never see a half-written file).
+func TestWorkerStatusSidecar_RecordsCurrentBeadAttemptAndLastActivity(t *testing.T) {
+	store, first, _ := newExecuteLoopTestStore(t)
+	projectRoot := store.Dir
+	sessionID := "agent-loop-test-sidecar"
+
+	runtime := ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{
+		ProjectRoot: projectRoot,
+		SessionID:   sessionID,
+	}
+	release, done := runBlockedHeartbeatLoopWithRuntime(t, store, 5*time.Millisecond, runtime)
+
+	// Wait for the sidecar to record the claimed bead with a non-zero
+	// last_activity_at — i.e. at least one tick has fired post-claim.
+	require.Eventually(t, func() bool {
+		rec, err := workerstatus.ReadLiveness(projectRoot, sessionID)
+		if err != nil {
+			return false
+		}
+		return rec.CurrentBead == first.ID && !rec.LastActivityAt.IsZero()
+	}, time.Second, 5*time.Millisecond, "sidecar must reflect the claimed bead within a few ticks")
+
+	firstSnap, err := workerstatus.ReadLiveness(projectRoot, sessionID)
+	require.NoError(t, err)
+
+	// Wait for several more ticks so last_activity_at advances at least twice
+	// past firstSnap, proving the sidecar is updated on every tick.
+	require.Eventually(t, func() bool {
+		rec, err := workerstatus.ReadLiveness(projectRoot, sessionID)
+		if err != nil {
+			return false
+		}
+		return rec.LastActivityAt.After(firstSnap.LastActivityAt)
+	}, time.Second, 5*time.Millisecond, "sidecar last_activity_at must advance on each tick")
+
+	rec, err := workerstatus.ReadLiveness(projectRoot, sessionID)
+	require.NoError(t, err)
+
+	require.Equal(t, sessionID, rec.WorkerID, "sidecar must record the worker_id")
+	require.Equal(t, first.ID, rec.CurrentBead, "sidecar must record the current bead")
+	require.NotEmpty(t, rec.AttemptID, "sidecar must record a (provisional) attempt id")
+	require.Equal(t, string(work.PhaseRunning), rec.Phase, "sidecar must record the current phase")
+	require.False(t, rec.LastActivityAt.IsZero(), "sidecar must record last_activity_at")
+	require.Equal(t, os.Getpid(), rec.PID, "sidecar must record the worker pid when available")
+
+	release()
+	require.NoError(t, <-done)
+}
